title: mahonia repens var. rotundifolia
intro: Mahonia repens var. rotundifolia, also known as Oregon grape or creeping barberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It possesses medicinal properties due to the presence of berberine, a bioactive alkaloid that exhibits antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Mahonia repens var. rotundifolia include its potential to aid in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and support the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and wellness.
